title: Correa cotinifolia
intro: Correa cotinifolia, commonly known as the Queensland Correa, is a flowering plant species native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Correa cotinifolia include coriariin, coriariol, and other phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Correa cotinifolia include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
